lblRotationBlock: 📱 Please rotate your device to portrait mode
sessionCompleted: Session complete! You completed {limit} attempts. Take a short break to rest your eyes.
sessionMastered: Incredible focus! Your visual cortex has reached its maximum plastic saturation for today. You have successfully mastered Stage 5 at ultra-low contrast! Stop here to lock in these results and let your eyes rest. Outstanding job!
secAboutText: Amblyopia is a neurological impairment of the primary visual cortex (V1) characterized by active binocular suppression of the weaker eye's signals. Gabor patches correspond mathematically to the receptive fields of V1 simple cells. Forcing the amblyopic eye to resolve their orientation at the absolute contrast threshold drives synaptic remodeling (Hebbian plasticity) and restores signal conduction.
secInstructionsText:
• Indications: Classic Occlusion and Cortical Speed Blitz protocols.
  • Preparation: Patch the dominant (strong) eye completely. Train only the amblyopic eye.
  • Positioning: Position the device at a stable arm's length (50–70 cm).
  • Fixation: Gaze strictly at the central cross (+). Use peripheral attention to resolve the tilt without moving your eye.
secLevelsList:
• Indications: Binocular Balance, Flicker Resonance, and Parafoveal Capture.
  • Preparation: Do not patch either eye. Wear calibrated Red-Cyan 3D glasses.
  • Worth 4-Dot Test: The calibration screen evaluates your character of vision. Seeing both 'L' (Red) and 'R' (Cyan) simultaneously indicates active binocular fusion. If one letter disappears, your visual cortex is actively suppressing that channel.
  • Suppression Calibration: While looking at the L/R letters, press ArrowUp/Down (or W/S / swipe vertically). This attenuates the strong eye's contrast. Stop when both letters appear with equal subjective intensity to establish your precise binocular suppression threshold.
secPresetsText:
• 🩹 Classic Occlusion: Monocular stimulation. Depriving the healthy eye forces the visual cortex to allocate 100% of attentional and metabolic resources to decoding Gabor signals from the weaker fovea.
  • ⚡ Cortical Speed Blitz: Instant feed-forward training. Ultra-short 100ms flashes prevent compensatory saccadic eye movements, forcing V1 simple cells to decode visual targets reflexively.
  • 🕶️ Binocular Balance (3D): Dichoptic balance restoration. Attenuating contrast for the dominant channel under 3D glasses bypasses active cortical suppression, forcing the brain to accept and integrate binocular inputs.
  • 🌀 Flicker Resonance (3D): Suppressive barrier override. Pulsating the Gabor target at 10 Hz (the brain's alpha attentional frequency) resonant-activates V1 neurons, disrupting chronic dominant-eye suppression.
  • 🎯 Parafoveal Capture (3D): Retinal map calibration. Shifting Gabor targets to peripheral eccentricity trains correct foveal spatial localization and counters eccentric fixation.
  • ⚙️ Adaptive Spacing (Crowding vs. Facilitation): Adjusting the flanker distance coefficient:
    • Close Spacing (2x): Simulates the Visual Crowding Effect to challenge lateral inhibition and improve central resolution.
    • Far Spacing (4x): Triggers Lateral Facilitation, serving as a neural amplifier to help resolve fine stripes at ultra-low contrast thresholds.
  • 🧲 Synoptophore (3D): Orthoptics & motor vergence. Treating strabismus and unstable fixation. Aligning targets to your subjective squint angle establishes sensory fusion. The system then slowly pulls targets back to the geometric center, forcing extraocular muscles to contract to maintain alignment (motor vergence).
secRecommendationsText:
• 1. Focus ONLY on the central cross (+): Never chase the Gabor patch with your gaze. Resolving the tilt using your foveal vision trains receptive fields and corrects ocular misalignment.
  • 2. The 15-Minute Rule: Train daily for 100–150 attempts (approx. 15 minutes). Rest is critical for consolidating visual memory; overtraining only causes muscle fatigue.
  • 3. Listen to Your Body: Stop immediately if your eyes water or feel painful. Always prioritize comfort.
helpFlankerDistance: Adjusts flanker gap. Spacing at 2x sigma challenges spatial crowding, while spacing at 4x sigma acts as a neural amplifier to help resolve ultra-low contrasts.
helpPermanentCross: Retains a faded central cross during the flash to stabilize gaze and counter eccentric fixation drift.
helpTimerLimit: Automatically halts the session and prompts a rest break to prevent ocular muscle fatigue and accommodative spasm.
sessionTimerCompleted: Outstanding focus! Your timer has finished. Please look away at a distant object for 20 seconds to relax your ciliary muscles before continuing.
helpAutonext: Automatically triggers the next flash 1 second after your answer, enabling a seamless training flow without extra clicks.
helpSessionLimit: Sets a hard cap on the number of attempts for Gabor mode to strictly control visual workload.
descModeExplanation: Preset guidelines: Select an active protocol as a starting template. Modify any checkbox below to seamlessly transition into 'Custom' configuration mode.
helpPresetMode: Clinical pre-sets (active macros) designed for binocular, peripheral, or rapid temporal stimulation.
helpStartLevel: Initial spatial frequency. Higher stages have ultra-fine lines requiring greater visual acuity.
helpFlashDuration: Exposure duration. Short flashes prevent compensatory eye movements (saccades) and force instant processing.
helpStageAdvance: Allows the program to automatically advance or downgrade stages based on your accuracy streak.
helpPeripheral: Randomly shifts the target to train parafoveal spatial localization and peripheral attentional fields.
helpCrowding: Surrounds the target Gabor with side distractors to challenge lateral suppression and the crowding effect.
helpLowContrast: Allows the adaptive contrast staircase to descend down to the extreme 1% threshold instead of 5%.
helpWideVariance: Randomizes the line density and visual size of the Gabor patch with every flash to prevent neural frequency adaptation.
helpShapeVariance: Randomly squashes the Gabor patch into an ellipse to challenge orientation selectivity and counter astigmatism.
helpStatic: The Gabor patch stays visible indefinitely until you choose an answer (no auto-hiding).
helpFlicker: Pulsates the target at 10 Hz (Alpha-resonance) to bypass chronic cortical suppression of the weak eye.
helpAnaglyph: Splits red/cyan color channels for dichoptic training. Requires Red-Cyan 3D glasses.
helpFusionLock: Renders a zero-disparity frame and corner brackets to stabilize eye alignment and prevent dominant-eye suppression.
helpOrthogonal: Rotates flanking bars perpendicular to the target, creating distinct orientation boundaries.
helpDynamic: Runs a continuous motion wave inside the flankers to saturate dominant eye motion channels.
lblSynopStatusAlign: Look STRICTLY at the center dot! Use peripheral vision and drag the screen (or WASD keys/tap canvas edges) to align the targets.
lblSynopStatusTraining: Fusion locked! Muscles are slowly pulling the eye back to center... Maintain alignment!
synopBreakText: Great attempt! You held the binocular fusion down to {current} px offset (started at {start} px). Your extraocular muscles successfully compensated for {percent}% of the strabismus deviation! With daily practice, maintaining focus will become effortless. Take a short break.
synopSuccessText: Outstanding achievement! You have fully pulled your eyes' visual axes to the geometric center (0 pixels) without losing fusion. Your binocular vergence has completely compensated for the deviation. Rest for 10 seconds and repeat to consolidate the muscle memory!
helpSynopPullSpeed: Determines how fast the software pulls targets back to true center (0,0). Slower speeds prevent fusion breaks and are gentler on weak extraocular muscles.
helpTargetType: Selects the central sensory anchor. 'Ring & Dot' is forgiving, while 'Cross & Square' requires stricter vertical and horizontal alignment.
helpSynopLazyGrid: Projects dashed guidelines from the corners converging on the center of the displaced lazy eye target.
helpSynopStrongGrid: Projects dashed guidelines from the corners converging on the static foveal center of the strong eye.
helpTargetSize: Foveal (Small) size forces the brain to lock with extreme sub-pixel precision on micro-deviations.
synopWarnAlign: Please shift the target first to match your deviation!
helpSynopFlicker: Pulsates the lazy eye target at 10 Hz (Alpha-resonance) to completely override top-down foveal suppression.
helpSynopLockY: Locks the vertical axis to zero. Ideal for treating standard horizontal strabismus without fat-finger errors.
helpSynopLockX: Locks the horizontal axis to zero. Ideal for treating vertical strabismus without fat-finger errors.
msgImportConfirm: Are you sure you want to import {count} training sessions into the active profile "{name}"?
msgImportSuccess: Successfully imported {count} training sessions into the active profile!
msgImportError: Failed to parse CSV file. Please make sure the format is valid and untouched.
chartPlaceholder: Complete at least 2 sessions to plot your contrast sensitivity trend line.
msgConfirmDelete: Delete profile "{name}"? This will cascaded-delete all their historical training sessions!
msgCannotDeleteLast: Cannot delete the only remaining patient profile in the database!
msgConfirmClear: Completely purge all training history for this patient? This action is permanent!
chartLegend: 📈 Progress Trend: Upward slope represents clinical improvement in cortical sensitivity (threshold reduction).
synopChartLegend: 📈 Vergence Progress: Upward slope represents clinical alignment toward 0.0Δ. Last 10 sessions.
